hints.determination: Channel your energy into one single priority to avoid scattering.
hints.influence: Express your idea in one clear sentence before convincing the group.
hints.stabilite: Protect your rhythm with a short but consistent routine.
hints.rigueur: Aim for useful precision, not perfection.

writingBenefits.determination: Writing channels your energy: you move from strong impulse to useful action.
writingBenefits.influence: Writing clarifies your message before discussion, so you are better understood and more impactful.
writingBenefits.stabilite: Writing stabilizes your rhythm: you stay consistent even when the week gets heavy.
writingBenefits.rigueur: Writing sorts essentials from details: you keep quality without getting stuck in perfection.

starters.determination: The decisive action I choose today (and why) is...
starters.influence: The message I want to communicate clearly today is...
starters.stabilite: The short ritual I protect to stay steady this week is...
starters.rigueur: The quality level that is enough to move forward today is...

determination: Day 1: define one priority decision to make.
Day 2: take action within 20 minutes max.
Day 3: measure the real impact of that choice.
influence: Day 1: phrase your idea in one clear sentence.
Day 2: share it with one key person and listen to feedback.
Day 3: adjust your message for more clarity.
stabilite: Day 2: protect this routine despite surprises.
Day 3: note effects on your calm and consistency.
rigueur: Day 1: define one simple quality standard.
Day 2: apply it to one concrete task.
Day 3: decide what to simplify to move faster.

wellbeingRadar.title: Psychological well-being
wellbeingRadar.subtitle: Quickly understand what drains you, what supports you, and where to recover momentum.
wellbeingRadar.emptyQuickOption: Quick option: complete the guided path in 2 minutes.
wellbeingRadar.emptyOutcome: Get a clear reading of your energy, calm, and balance levels.
wellbeingRadar.emptyAnalyzeRequirement: Automatic page analysis needs at least 5 non-encrypted entries.
wellbeingRadar.applyPlan: Apply this plan in my journal

hints.acceptationDeSoi: Speak to yourself like a close friend: fair, not harsh.
hints.developpementPersonnel: Pick one concrete micro-learning goal for this week.
hints.sensDeLaVie: Write one action that moves you closer to what matters.
hints.maitriseEnvironnement: Break your day into one single priority.
hints.autonomie: Make one simple decision that truly feels like you.
hints.relationsPositives: Send one sincere message to a key person in your life.

writingBenefits.acceptationDeSoi: Regular writing reduces self-criticism and helps you recover a kinder inner voice.
writingBenefits.developpementPersonnel: Writing anchors learning, accelerates growth, and prevents the feeling of stagnation.
writingBenefits.sensDeLaVie: Writing links your actions to your values, bringing direction and reducing mental fog.
writingBenefits.maitriseEnvironnement: Writing turns mental chaos into concrete priorities, so you regain control of your day.
writingBenefits.autonomie: Writing recenters you on what you truly choose, not on outside pressure.
writingBenefits.relationsPositives: Writing clarifies relational needs and makes your conversations calmer and more authentic.

starters.acceptationDeSoi: Today, one thing I did well (even a small one) is...
starters.developpementPersonnel: This week, what I learned about myself and want to keep is...
starters.sensDeLaVie: What truly matters to me right now, and the action that follows, is...
starters.maitriseEnvironnement: To regain control today, my single top priority is...
starters.autonomie: The decision I make for myself today, without betraying myself, is...
starters.relationsPositives: The relationship I want to nurture this week, and how I'll do it, is...

acceptationDeSoi: Day 1: note one real strength you used today.
Day 2: rewrite one self-criticism into a fairer sentence.
Day 3: write one concrete proof of recent progress.
developpementPersonnel: Day 2: apply it in a real situation.
Day 3: write what changed in your week.
sensDeLaVie: Day 1: write what matters most to you right now.
Day 2: choose one action aligned with that priority.
Day 3: reflect on how it felt.
maitriseEnvironnement: Day 1: define one top priority for tomorrow.
autonomie: Day 1: make one short decision based on your own criteria.
Day 2: decline one non-priority request clearly.
Day 3: note what this choice gave you.
relationsPositives: Day 1: send a gratitude message to one key person.
Day 2: ask one sincere question to someone close.
Day 3: write what this relationship truly gives you.

0.hint: Write without filtering. Regain clarity with gentle reflection.
0.detail: Just write what's on your mind, without filtering.
0.quote: Writing is a way of talking without being interrupted.
0.author: Jules Renard

1.hint: When you're ruminating, write down what keeps looping.
1.detail: Getting words out helps your mind slow down.
1.quote: I write to find out what I think.
1.author: Joan Didion

2.hint: If you're carrying too much, set it all down here, line by line.
2.detail: You don't need to write perfectly, just write honestly.
2.quote: There is no greater agony than bearing an untold story inside you.
2.author: Maya Angelou

3.hint: When you doubt yourself, return to the facts and your feelings.
3.detail: A few lines can give you solid ground again.
3.quote: One word at a time — that's the power.
3.author: Margaret Atwood

4.hint: When inner overload rises, this space helps you sort what matters.
4.detail: You can set down what feels tangled and notice what asks for clarity.
4.quote: We write to taste life twice.
4.author: Anaïs Nin

5.hint: After a heavy day, write what stayed with you.
5.detail: Even 2 minutes of reflection can make a pattern easier to see.
5.quote: Writing is a form of therapy.
5.author: Graham Greene

6.hint: When everything is foggy, one clear sentence is enough to start again.
6.detail: Start small: one fact, one emotion, one need.
6.quote: Writing is a way of talking without being interrupted.
6.author: Jules Renard

7.hint: When your mind speeds up, slow down with simple words.
7.detail: Here, you can set things down without judgment.
7.quote: I write to find out what I think.
7.author: Joan Didion

questions.q: What you are looking for most right now is:
When you have to choose, you prioritize:

D.title: The Pioneer
D.description: You move quickly and decide decisively. Aurum helps channel that energy into clearer reflection and next steps.

I.title: The Connector
I.description: You are guided by connection and emotion. Aurum helps turn what you feel into something clearer and easier to express.

S.title: The Anchor
S.description: You seek steadiness and calm. Aurum gives you a private rhythm for slower, deeper reflection.

C.title: The Architect
C.description: You like to understand before acting. Aurum helps structure what you notice and what keeps returning.

MIXTE.title: Mixed profile • The Balancer
MIXTE.description: You combine several strengths. Aurum helps you notice how your inner patterns shift across situations.

studySection.title: Writing helps. Aurum helps you understand what keeps returning.
studySection.subtitle: Privacy protects your words. Aurum’s guided reflection helps turn them into emotional clarity and recurring patterns you can recognize over time.
studySection.cta: Create my account and start today

studyCards.title: writing about a stressful event can reduce physical symptoms?
studyCards.body: In a foundational study, people who wrote about a difficult event showed better health indicators.
studyCards.example: Practical example: 10 minutes at night to put down what weighs on you instead of ruminating in bed.

studyCards.title: putting emotions into words can improve your rebound?
studyCards.body: A study on people facing job loss found a stronger return to action among those who wrote regularly.
studyCards.example: Practical example: write three times a week about what you feel, then define the next realistic step.

studyCards.title: systematic reviews confirm gains in well-being?
studyCards.body: Recent syntheses show a positive effect of journaling on stress and psychological well-being.
studyCards.example: Practical example: a five-minute daily routine can already change your level of clarity.

useCases.title: Common moments when writing can restore clarity
useCases.subtitle: Concrete ways private reflection can untangle what you feel, reduce inner overload, and reveal what keeps returning.
useCases.note: Grounded in research on expressive writing and psychological well-being

cards.title: Your mind keeps spinning at night
cards.body: Write down what keeps looping. In practice, three lines before bed (fact, emotion, need) can already soothe your mind.

cards.title: You speak to yourself too harshly
cards.body: When the inner voice says “I am useless,” write a fairer version: “I am moving forward step by step.” It helps rebuild confidence.

cards.title: Everything feels tangled in your head
cards.body: Write one idea per line. You turn mental confusion into clear points, then pick one small step for today.

cards.title: You keep it all in until you explode
cards.body: A few regular lines are better than one rare long session. You decompress before saturation and regain calm.

cards.title: You do not know where to begin
cards.body: Simple rule: start with one fact, one emotion, one need. That is enough to begin without pressure, even in two minutes.

cards.title: Your task list is suffocating you
cards.body: Set a five-minute timer and write without editing: facts, emotions, needs. This short routine gives your mind some air back.

cards.title: After an argument, your mind replays the scene
cards.body: Write the facts, what you felt, and what you need now. You step out of the mental movie and come back to yourself.

cards.title: You feel drained without knowing why
cards.body: Write five lines at the end of the day: what drained your energy, and what gave some back. You regain reference points for tomorrow.

cards.title: You are stuck on an important decision
cards.body: Split the page in two columns: what you gain, what you lose. Putting thoughts in front of you often reveals the real choice.

scientificProof.title: This is not magic. It is an observed effect.
scientificProof.subtitle: Researchers have examined the power of writing. Their studies surfaced some striking findings:

discoveries.label: Finding #1:
discoveries.body: Participants who took time to write about their worries saw their medical visits drop significantly

discoveries.label: Finding #2:
discoveries.body: In another study on people who had lost their jobs, those who wrote about their emotions were twice as likely to find work again

problem.title: What does inner overload feel like?
problem.body: Thoughts keep circling, feelings blur together, and it becomes harder to see what matters. Writing makes those signals easier to notice.

solution.title: Writing slows things down enough to be seen.
solution.body: Private writing gives your thoughts somewhere to land. Aurum adds gentle guided reflection so clarity and recurring patterns can emerge over time.

trust.title: A sanctuary where you remain in control.
trust.subtitle: Aurum was designed around one simple idea: your inner world belongs to you alone. Not even to us.

cards.title: Absolute privacy
cards.body: Your thoughts are encrypted (AES-256) directly on your device. Even we cannot read your private notes.

cards.title: Guaranteed anonymity
cards.body: No personal identity is tied to your writing. Aurum's analysis engine operates locally on your device.

cards.title: Right to be forgotten
cards.body: You remain the 100% owner of your data. Export your journals or delete your account in one click, without delay.

trust.manifestoBody: "We do not sell advertising. We do not sell your data. We protect a private space for clarity, reflection, and self-understanding. Your journal is not a product. It is your inner ground."

finalCta.subtitle: You enter a private reflection space designed to help you write regularly, understand what keeps returning, and regain clarity over time.

cards.title: writing
cards.body: A calm space to write freely, without distraction or self-censorship.

cards.title: reflection
cards.body: Guided reflection that helps recurring themes, emotional rhythms, and unmet needs become visible over time.

cards.title: privacy
cards.body: Your words stay encrypted and protected by default.

cards.title: continuity
cards.body: Your reflection deepens over time, page after page, without losing continuity.

faqs.question: Who can read my data?
faqs.answer: No one. We use an “Admin-Blind” architecture with client-side AES-256 encryption. Your entries are encrypted with your private key before being sent. Technically, even with total server access, your writing cannot be decrypted without your password.

faqs.question: How can private writing help me regain clarity at night?
faqs.answer: Write three lines before bed: what feels heavy, what you feel, and what you need tomorrow. The goal is to move looping thoughts onto the page so clarity can return.

faqs.question: How can I reduce inner overload in 5 minutes?
faqs.answer: Set a five-minute timer and write without editing: facts, emotions, needs. This short reflection helps reduce inner overload and regain clarity, even on a crowded day.

faqs.question: How can reflection help me step out of overthinking?
faqs.answer: When everything spins in your head, write one thought per line. Reflection turns mental noise into something you can see, name, and act on.

faqs.question: Can reflective writing improve emotional clarity?
faqs.answer: Reflective writing can improve emotional clarity over time by helping you name what you feel, notice what repeats, and create some distance from mental noise.

faqs.question: What private reflection app actually protects my writing?
faqs.answer: Choose a reflection app with end-to-end encryption, no advertising, and no public exposure of your writing. On Aurum, your words stay in a private space built for clarity and pattern discovery.

faqs.question: How much does Aurum cost?
faqs.answer: Aurum offers a monthly plan at 13€/month and a yearly plan at 129€/year (2 months free).
